video_title: Top #DDD Pork Belly Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Sunday Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here on Tybee Island, which is on US Highway 80. It comes right out of Savannah. Now, this place started as an ice cream shop, but this guy is a trained chef, and he says, I'm going to take it a different direction. You're going to love the name, though. This is Sunday Cafe. What are up, buffalo shirt nachos? It really is the local hot spot. And it's cooked up by a Tybee native, AJ Baker, who couldn't leave well enough alone with just an ice cream shop, so he flexed his culinary muscle.

dishes.dish_name: Down-South Grilled Cheese with Pork Belly
dishes.description: Brined and smoked pork belly, collard greens, Alabama white sauce, buttermilk-dredged fried chicken breast, American cheese, served on toasted bread.
dishes.guy_response: The fried chicken is spot on. The pork belly is so tender and delicious. The collard greens are money. The Thai bee white sauce is delicious. Acid and fat, that little bit of that horseradish coming through, outstanding. I mean, like, really a great sandwich.

restaurants.name: Kobe Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: Talk about real deal. Got a husband and wife team. He decides to pack up, move to Japan, and study ramen, then come back and open up shop. That's real deal. This is Kobe Japanese Restaurant.

dishes.description: Ramen with a rich mother broth made from pork bones, back skin, ears, vegetables, and apples, served with fried and braised tonpuro pork belly, shoyu base, spinach, noodles, bok choy, and onion oil.
dishes.guy_response: First and foremost, the broth. Good collagen. Silkiness. You get the velvet on the tongue. Dynamite noodle. Cooked perfectly. The pork's the star. It's tender. A ton of flavor. Beautiful that you have the bok choy and the spinach. It's every bit of fantastic. Perfect. Lights out, man.

restaurants.name: Vida Cantina
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're here in Portsmouth, New Hampshire. And I finally get the job at Triple D car. So I figured, why not take Hunter to get some real-deal Mexican food here on the East Coast? Mexican food? East Coast? He's from Southern California. Oh, so he's got to be legit. This is Vida Cantina.

dishes.description: Homemade tortillas made from Indian corn, topped with eight-day cured and lard-cooked pork belly, jicama slaw, mango habanero salsa, cotija cheese, and edible flowers.
dishes.guy_response: That is awesome. The freshest little tortilla totally makes it. I love the slaw. That pork belly's just cooked perfectly, too. Yeah, I could eat that all day. So the tortilla, you can taste that mixed them all. You can taste how fresh it is. There's fantastic texture in it. The tortilla, I like that little saltiness. The pork is really the note that sings, and you do it in such a nice, fat slab that you're really getting some meaty texture in it. If you told me I was getting this at a contemporary Mexican restaurant in San Diego, I would believe you. Fantastic.

restaurants.name: Fat Joy Asian American Fair
restaurants.guy_intro: I think everybody knows by now I went to college here in Las Vegas, and I'm about a half mile off the Strip. Now, I drove by this place a million times. Never stopped in here. It's called the Eureka Casino, and boy, did I miss out. This is the home of Fat Joy Asian American Fair.

dishes.description: Marinated and baked pork belly, compressed and seared, served in steamed buns with hoisin sauce, preserved mustard greens, crushed peanuts, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Great steamed bun. Nice, tender, nice and light. The pork is tender, sweet. I like the counterbalance with the mustard green. Really good, man. Awesome. A real deal. Nice. All right. Pork belly bell. Luscious and delicious and outstanding. It's like the ultimate slider, right? It's like super-sized bacon.

restaurants.name: Jamaica Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: You ever find yourself cruising around town thinking, well, I want some Jamaican food. You know, like some stewed oxtail or maybe some curried goat, or better yet, some stewed peas with pigtail. Well, if you're in Kendall, Florida, you're in luck. You got to come and check out this joint, Jamaica Kitchen.

dishes.dish_name: Pork Belly with Pickled Mustard Green
dishes.description: Pork belly boiled with sugar, salt, mushroom soy, bean sauce, oyster sauce, ginger, green onions, white onions, garlic, sesame oil, and black beans, then simmered with pickled mustard greens.
dishes.guy_response: I'm not alive. See the sauce on that? Money. Big, deep, rich flavor. That's good.

restaurants.name: Belly and Snout
restaurants.guy_intro: There are so many different pockets of culinary culture throughout Los Angeles. Little Italy, Chinatown, just to name a few. But back in 2016, I found a Filipino fusion spot smack dab in the middle of Koreatown. Now, they've left the city for the Inland Empire, but their loyal customers are more than willing to make the drive. This is Belly and Snout.

dishes.description: Beef hot dog topped with ground pork snout and pork belly, bell peppers, jalapenos, red onions, garlic crema, pickled red onions, sliced red radish, green onions, calamansi, and chicharron.
dishes.guy_response: It eats bigger than probably any hot dog I've ever had. But that seasick right on top, it's crunchy. It's tender. I'd want a whole sandwich of that.

restaurants.name: Koda Bakery Vietnamese Deli and Tofu House
restaurants.guy_intro: So on Triple D, I've been there. I've done that. I've met them. I've heard their story. And I've tasted just about every type of menu that there is. Well, I'm here in Albuquerque, New Mexico. And that might get broken today. This is a story about an immigrant family like I've never heard before. They're doing a menu that I've, well, I don't think I've quite seen before. This one's unique. This is Koda Bakery Vietnamese Deli and Tofu House.

dishes.description: House-made baguette filled with garlic mayo, pate, pork belly, head ham, jambon, Vietnamese ham, soy sauce, pickled carrots and daikon, cucumber, jalapenos, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Amazing. This is probably one of the most remarkable sandwiches I've had a chance to try. This is a trophy of success for all your hard work. Oh my goodness. Outstanding. Thank you. Banh mi look real for you. The whole sandwich is really fresh and tasty. It has the vegetables. It has the butter on the baguette and the baguettes here. So it's better than pretty much any sandwich you can get.

restaurants.name: The Pickle Jar Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Falmouth, Massachusetts, right between, let me get this right, Plymouth and Hyannis. In a small little town that during the off season, they've got like 200,000 people here. In the off season, it's more like 30,000. Now, I'm on the main drag to check out a funky little joint where two couples are kind of in a pickle. Well, not exactly. This is the Pickle Jar Kitchen.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced pickles, dredged and perfectly seasoned, then fried until crispy.
dishes.guy_response: This right here, you could have dredged in sand. I would eat it. It's thin, balanced flavor, perfectly seasoned. And the crunch. Oh. I haven't even done this yet. Oh, yeah. That gets ridiculous right there. Good. Outrageous. Well, I think they're like potato chips. You do not eat just one.

dishes.dish_name: Five Chili Barbecue Pork Open Face
dishes.description: Braised pork butt rubbed with a complex spice blend, served open-face on a cheddar scallion scone with braising liquid, Spanish onion, various peppers, barbecue sauce, smoky cheddar sauce, and house pickled red onion.
dishes.guy_response: It's got a lot going on. With the fresh-baked scone, you make the sauce. A little bit of the pickled onion. The combo of the sofrito. Sofrito. And the cheese sauce that put this over the top. That's sophisticated right there, man. Open face is all set. The scone and pulled pork together, incredible. It's just perfect.

restaurants.name: Pelican Seafood Market and Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Ottawa, Canada, in an area known as Alta Vista. And I'm about 10 minutes from the downtown area. And you know what we don't even see more of? Freestanding seafood markets. But these folks at Alta Vista, they got it going on. They got their own joint. It's Pelican Seafood Market and Grill.

dishes.description: Seared scallops and braised pork belly (with onions, carrots, celery, garlic, thyme, apple cider), served with parsnip puree, jus, beets, snap peas, Brussels sprouts, green curly kale, and waffle fries.
dishes.guy_response: That's a really good scallop, man. You cooked it perfectly. Super tender. There's great crust. The jus is a really nice complement to it. That parmesan. Parsnip puree. The time that you all are taking here to braise the belly and to serve it up with the seasonal veg carries it through. Nice job, man. Really nice job. Scallops going in. Just lovely and tender and moist and succulent. The pork belly with a little bit of apple cider gives it that nice crispiness and that subtle, dry flavor.
